items.kicker: For Eco-6810, Computational Thinking and Artificial Intelligence for Economists, Ashoka University
items.title: Decision under Uncertainty: Squad Allocation and Match Simulation Engine
items.text: Probabilistic ball-by-ball Indian Premier League simulation engine trained on 288,051 deliveries across 1,212 matches from 2008 to 2026 and 11 outcome classes. Its modern-profile XGBoost model achieved 1.8293 log-loss versus a 1.8767 empirical baseline, about a 2.5% improvement. The Streamlit app supports recency-weighted and lifetime modes, venue and matchup calibration, batting-position effectiveness, phase-aware bowling allocation, repeated simulations, and complete projected scorecards for comparing alternative elevens.

items.kicker: For Eco-3401, Data Analytics and Machine Learning, Ashoka University
items.title: Forecasting India's Gross Value Added: Agriculture and Services Sectors
items.text: Dual forecasting pipeline using Sarimax and a machine learning ensemble with XGBoost, LightGBM, and Ridge, weighted 0.4:0.4:0.2, on 58 quarterly observations from financial year 2011-12 to 2025-26. Implemented expanding-window cross-validation and Moving Block Bootstrap with 1,000 trajectories. Ensemble cross-validation mean absolute percentage error was about 4.4% for agriculture and 3.0% for services, outperforming standalone Sarimax on both sectors.

items.title: Contractualisation and Wage Differences in Indian Manufacturing
items.text: Used firm-level Annual Survey of Industries 2023-24 data with more than 41,700 observations to test whether contractualisation suppresses wages in organised manufacturing. Instrumental variable and two-stage least squares estimation instrumented firm-level contractualisation with peer-average contractualisation at the 5-digit industrial classification level. A 1 percentage point rise in contractualisation reduced daily wages by Rs. 2.34, significant at 1%; ordinary least squares underestimated this effect by about 42.8%.

items.kicker: With Azad Foundation and Sabar Institute · 2024
items.title: Access to Public Toilets for Women and Trans/Queer Persons in Kolkata
items.text: One of Kolkata's largest gendered sanitation studies. 7,578 cis women and 38 trans/queer persons were surveyed by more than 300 trained student investigators from eight colleges and two universities. Key findings: 72% of women paid to access public toilets; 53% reported absent locks; 38% of trans/queer respondents reported harassment; 51% felt inadequate sanitation affected their ability to work and earn. The study received extensive media coverage and contributed to policy discussions on gender-sensitive sanitation and women's labour-force participation.

items.title: Migration Study in West Bengal
items.text: Large-scale empirical study of out-migration based on more than 7,500 household-level observations. Documented migrant profiles, destination choices, occupational mobility, remittance behaviour, and welfare access, addressing a major data gap on internal migration, particularly among economically vulnerable and minority households.

items.kicker: Calcutta Research Group and Know Your Neighbour · 2025
items.title: Continuing Persecution of Bengali-Speaking Migrant Workers
items.text: Research and advocacy initiative documenting harassment, detention, and citizenship-related vulnerability of Bengali-speaking migrant workers across Indian states. Compiled field evidence, incident reports, and legal references. The press intervention received significant media and policy attention.

items.kicker: Know Your Neighbour and Sabar Institute · 2025
items.title: Status of Education in Metiabruz, Kolkata
items.text: Neighbourhood-level education study based on more than 2,500 observations covering enrolment, dropout risks, gendered constraints, and household expenditure on education in a marginalised urban minority locality. Produced actionable evidence for targeted scholarships and school-support programmes.

items.title: Reading Habits in Middle Schoolers
items.text: School-based study covering more than 4,300 students examining non-curricular reading, library access, home reading environments, and screen exposure. Generated evidence for low-cost school interventions to strengthen reading culture and foundational literacy.

items.title: Summer incubatee, Infoedge Centre for Entrepreneurship
items.text: I have been selected as a summer incubatee for the Infoedge Centre for Entrepreneurship for Ashoka University's summer incubation programme, where I am developing my machine learning decision simulator around Indian Premier League teams: CricPredAI. Check it out, simulate, have fun, and send any feedback or criticism you might have.

items.title: Quizzing, debate, and academic competitions
items.text:
I have been extremely active in extracurricular activities with 35+ awards across quizzes, debates, research paper competitions, data analyses, case study competitions, and so on. Having been an avid quizzer for almost 15 years now, I have won awards representing my school, college, and university.

I was the founder and secretary of the quiz club at my university, Dead Quizzers Society. I am also currently the president of the Quizzing Club at Ashoka University. I host regular quizzes.
